content: AVSpeechSynthesizer: a SINGLE synthesizer instance enqueues speak() calls in order. Multiple synthesizer instances cause overlap. Stored property requirement: synthesizer must remain in memory until all utterances complete — local var is deallocated early. WARNING: some community reports indicate sequential ordering guarantee may be unreliable on recent macOS/iOS releases; the 2014 bug about pause/stop not working on queued utterances may indicate deeper ordering issues. Best approach: attempt simple sequential speak() calls (they work in practice for brief phrases); verify empirically on macOS 13-15. Fallback: use AVSpeechSynthesizerDelegate.speechSynthesizer(_:didFinish:) to chain sequential announcements. For D-02/D-03 pending announcement queue: multiple announce() calls from flushPendingAnnouncements() on main actor call speak() sequentially — single synthesizer should queue them in order.
